free_text: Neutron capture by the chromium isotopes
Enriched chromium oxide and natural chromium metal
The efficiency of the detector was proportional to the
total gamma ray energy release and was independent of
Thin (0.5 mm) Li-6 glass detector was used as
Modified version of Sullivan's Monte Carlo code
The normalization factor was obtained by comparing
value of the capture kernel from natural data with
Modified version of the ORNL-RPI Monte Carlo code
Capture yields on Cr-50,52,53,54 and nat received from
authors at NDS were stored on a special archive tapes
named EXFOR SUPPL NDS 001, but no longer available.

Two large s-wave resonances at 50.3 and 98.0 keV have
neighboring p-wave resonances of 50.2 and 95.2 keV,  3
respectively, and the parameters for these latter
resonances are affected by fit for s-wave resonance
and hence have larger uncertainties than normal.
